cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the movies. Let's see... The key here is to look at the alibis or the times when Leslie was seen doing other activities and then determine the gaps when Leslie might have been free to go to the movies. The movies are closed after 7pm, so whatever time Leslie went to the movies, it has to be before 7pm. Let's break down the information: 1. Leslie woke up at 6am. So she's definitely up by then, but we don't know what she did immediately after waking up. 2. Susan saw Leslie sitting on a rooftop from 6am to 7am. That means Leslie was on the rooftop during that hour. 3. Mary saw Leslie working at the office from 12pm to 2pm. So Leslie was at the office during that two-hour window. 4. Steven saw Leslie getting a coffee at the cafe from 2pm to 7pm. That's a five-hour span. Now, let's map out Leslie's schedule based on these observations: - 6am to 7am: On the rooftop (with Susan) - 7am to 12pm: Not reported to be doing anything specific. We don't have evidence of where Leslie was during this time. - 12pm to 2pm: At the office (with Mary) - 2pm to 7pm: At the cafe (with Steven) The movies are closed after 7pm, so Leslie had to go before that. The only time window with no reported activity is from 7am to 12pm. During this time, we don't have any alibi for Leslie. So this is the only window when she could have gone to the movies. Let's check the answer options: (A) 7am to 12pm (B) 2pm to 7pm (C) 12pm to 2pm (D) 6am to 7am Option A matches our reasoning. Leslie was on the rooftop from 6am to 7am (so couldn't have gone to the movies then), was at the office from 12pm to 2pm, and at the cafe
